evidence: api/ package access modifiers are inconsistent: SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, RelationUtils, HttpApiUtils are package-private; ExploreResponseBuilder is public — no documented rationale for the asymmetry

Utility class naming: CqlUtils, HttpApiUtils, RelationUtils end in 'Utils'; LogDiceCalculator ends in 'Calculator'; no pattern drift from different modules, but a minor vocabulary inconsistency within the utils/ package

Model classes mix regular classes (ExplorationResult, ComparisonResult — with utility methods) and records (AdjectiveProfile, CoreCollocate, DiscoveredNoun, Edge, ExploreOptions) — the package-info.java explicitly documents the rationale, so this is not an undocumented outlier
All handler classes follow the same package-private pattern with no public constructors called from outside api/ — consistent internal access discipline

RelationType and PosGroup are in model/ package; RelationEdgeType is in exploration/ package — enum placement is inconsistent: two type-domain enums are in model/ but the edge-type enum is in exploration/ where it is most used

ExplorationResult and ComparisonResult use traditional class + getter methods; other result types (DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate) are records — mixing conventions in the same package, though package-info.java justifies this
Test class naming: all end in 'Test' suffix consistently; helper class GrammarConfigHelper does not end in Test — acceptable as it is a fixture, not a test class
BlackLabConllUIndexer in indexer/blacklab/ sub-package follows the same naming convention as ConlluConverter in the same package
convention_outlier.issues_preventing_higher_score: ExploreResponseBuilder is public while all its siblings in api/ are package-private; RelationEdgeType is in exploration/ while sibling type-domain enums RelationType and PosGroup are in model/ — two inconsistencies in type/access organization.

strengths: Package-info.java documents the mixed record/class convention in model/ with clear rationale — the asymmetry is intentional, not drift
All test classes follow the consistent SomeClassTest naming pattern
Handler classes consistently use package-private access with no public constructors, forming a coherent internal-API pattern
Consistent camelCase methods and PascalCase class names throughout — no snake_case islands
convention_outlier.issue_character: Issues are isolated to package-placement (RelationEdgeType in exploration/ vs model/) and access-modifier inconsistency (ExploreResponseBuilder public vs siblings package-private) — not a systemic naming drift but a pair of localized organizational outliers.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The codebase is well-organized with documented rationale for the one intentional mixed pattern (records vs classes in model/). The two deductions are the public ExploreResponseBuilder in an otherwise package-private api/ package, and the placement of RelationEdgeType in exploration/ when sibling enums live in model/. These are minor organizational mismatches that a new contributor would notice but could quickly learn to work with.

issues.summary: ExploreResponseBuilder is public while all sibling api/ handler classes are package-private
evidence: SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, RelationUtils, HttpApiUtils are all package-private (no public modifier)
ExploreResponseBuilder is only used within the api/ package (ExplorationHandlers calls it directly)
issues.suggestion: Change ExploreResponseBuilder to package-private to match the rest of the api/ package; if it is needed in tests, make it package-private and add test-scoped access via a test in the api/ package

issues.summary: RelationEdgeType is in exploration/ while sibling type-domain enums RelationType and PosGroup are in model/
evidence: RelationType.java and PosGroup.java are in model/
RelationEdgeType.java is in exploration/ — same conceptual category (domain enum) but different package
issues.suggestion: Move RelationEdgeType to model/ to be consistent with RelationType and PosGroup; update ExploreResponseBuilder and Edge imports

root_causes: ExploreResponseBuilder may have been public to allow testing from outside the api/ package, or was public by default when first created and never restricted
RelationEdgeType was placed in exploration/ because it was first used there, rather than alongside the other domain enums in model/
